=== Metadata ===
Effect version: 3.19.19

/.src/runEffectInsideEffect.ts(15,21): suggestion TS377024: `Effect.runSync` is called inside an existing Effect context. Here, the inner Effect can be used directly. effect(runEffectInsideEffect)
/.src/runEffectInsideEffect.ts(23,5): suggestion TS377025: `Effect.runPromise` is called inside an Effect with a separate runtime invocation. In this context, run child Effects with the surrounding runtime, which can be accessed through `Effect.runtime` and `Runtime.runPromise`. effect(runEffectInsideEffect)


==== /.src/runEffectInsideEffect.ts (2 errors) ====
    // @effect-v3
    import { Data, Effect } from "effect"
    
    class DebuggerError extends Data.TaggedError("DebuggerError")<{
      cause: unknown
    }> {}
    
    export const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const response = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
        try: () => fetch("http://localhost:9229"),
        catch: (e) => new DebuggerError({ cause: e })
      })
      const data = yield* Effect.promise(() => response.json())
    
      const websocket = Effect.runSync(Effect.sync(() => new WebSocket(data.url)))
                        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377024: `Effect.runSync` is called inside an existing Effect context. Here, the inner Effect can be used directly. effect(runEffectInsideEffect)
      //                        ^- do not runSync in here
    
      websocket.onmessage = (event) => {
        const check = Effect.tryPromise({
          try: () => fetch(event.data as string),
          catch: (e) => new DebuggerError({ cause: e })
        })
        Effect.runPromise(check)
        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377025: `Effect.runPromise` is called inside an Effect with a separate runtime invocation. In this context, run child Effects with the surrounding runtime, which can be accessed through `Effect.runtime` and `Runtime.runPromise`. effect(runEffectInsideEffect)
        //        ^- no runPromise, use a runtime instead
      }
    })
